text.title: Azure Architecture (Well-Architected + Landing Zones) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Greenfield deployment uses the legacy ESLZ Terraform; first AVM-required feature lands and team faces forced migration.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied an old reference architecture from before August 2026.
antipattern.fix: Switch to AVM + Bicep. If Terraform mandate exists, use the AVM Terraform port (community-maintained), not ESLZ.

antipattern.symptom: Hub-spoke deployed but service-to-service uses connection strings + key vault references; one Key Vault breach exposes everything.
antipattern.root-cause: Team treated 'network in private subnet' as the security boundary.
antipattern.fix: Add Managed Identity to every service. Enable PIM for human access. Service-principal secrets banned for new workloads.

antipattern.symptom: Privileged role assignments are permanent; compromise of one admin account = full tenant access.
antipattern.fix: Convert all Owner / User-Access-Admin role assignments to PIM-eligible. Activation window 8h; require approval.

antipattern.symptom: Public IPs created across subscriptions; resources in non-allowed regions; encryption disabled silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Policy initiative not assigned at MG level.
antipattern.fix: Assign the baseline initiative at platform MG. Verify daily via Policy Compliance Reports.
